title: Immunohistochemistry analysis
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1sxlo","text":"Primary monoclonal mouse anti human antibodies (all obtained from R\u0026D Systems, Inc. MN. USA): anti-human CXCR3 antibody (IgG1, clone49801, staining concentration of 10 micrograms/ml), anti-human CXCR1 mAb (IgG2A, clone 42705, staining concentration of 10 micrograms/ml), anti-human CXCR4 (IgG2A, clone 44708, staining concentration 10 micrograms/ml).\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gs5ix","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aaxna","text":"The mouse anti human mAb for HLA-G was previously described and kindly provided by Dr. Mike McMaster (mouse IgM, staining concentration of 10 micrograms/ml) [2].","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Perform immunohistochemistry for chemokine receptors CXCR1, CXCR3 and CXCR4 on first trimester human placental paraffin-embedded sections as previously described [1], with microwave antigen retrieval using sodium citrate buffer (Zymed Laboratory Inc, San- Francisco, CA, USA).
Stain with the same concentration of isotype match controls for each chemokine receptor on the same serial sections as controls.

Perform immunohistochemistry on decidual sections with NCR-Fc fusion proteins (prepared as previously described 3) by microwave heating the formalin-fixed, paraffin embedded tissue sections in citrate buffer in order to retrieve antigens.
Then stain sections with the different NCR-Fc fusion proteins or control-Fc (8 μg/ml, final concentration) followed by biotinylated-goat-anti-human-Fc (Jackson ImmunoResearch, West Grove, PA).
Detect using the avidin-biotin peroxidase complex method with a Vectastain kit (Vector Laboratories, Burlingame, CA).
Decidual NK cells regulate key developmental processes at the human fetal-maternal interface by Hanna, J. et al. published in Nature Medicine on 09 Jun, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nm1452
creator: Protocols Importer
